metadata.title: Roundup / glyphosate mass tort — Bayer-Monsanto, the IARC-vs-EPA science split, the 2026 SCOTUS preemption win, and the state liability shields (both sides, dated)
metadata.status: the verdicts, settlement figures, the IARC/EPA findings, the 9th-Circuit vacatur, the 25-Jun-2026 SCOTUS Durnell ruling, and the 2025 state shield laws are FACT (court opinions, agency records, news of record); the carcinogenicity question is genuinely UNSETTLED and presented BOTH ways with dates; neither the 'proven carcinogen' nor the 'proven safe' framing is adopted
metadata.frame: The glyphosate/Roundup mass tort and its 2026 turning point. Bayer inherited the liability when it bought Monsanto (2018). Bellwether losses + ~177,000 claims drove ~$11B+ in payouts/reserves. The science is split: IARC (WHO) calls glyphosate a 'probable carcinogen' (2A, 2015); the EPA says 'not likely' — but the 9th Circuit vacated that finding (2022). The pivot: on 25 Jun 2026 the Supreme Court (Monsanto v. Durnell, 7-2) held FIFRA preempts state failure-to-warn claims, gutting the tort's main theory — while Bayer still pursues a $7.25B class settlement. In parallel, Bayer-backed 2025 state laws (ND, GA) make an EPA label a complete defense. Extends the Liability_Engineering thesis with a THIRD shield mechanism (federal preemption + label-defense statutes), distinct from the bankruptcy two-step (J&J) and opioid releases. Attaches to Bayer/Monsanto, EPA, SCOTUS, Liability_Engineering.

the_tort: Glyphosate (the active ingredient in Roundup), introduced by Monsanto in 1974, became the world's most-used herbicide — and the basis of the Roundup-Ready seed system. After IARC's 2015 classification, plaintiffs alleged glyphosate caused non-Hodgkin lymphoma and that Monsanto failed to warn. Early bellwether LOSSES — Johnson (Aug 2018), Hardeman (2019, federal), Pilliod (2019) — produced large (later reduced) verdicts; the docket grew to ~177,000 claims, and Bayer (which acquired Monsanto in 2018 for ~$63B) paid/reserved roughly $11B+ to settle most while litigation continued with mixed verdicts. grade: fact (verdicts, claim counts, reserves).
the_science_split: Genuinely unsettled, presented both ways with dates: (A) IARC (the WHO cancer agency) classified glyphosate as a Group 2A 'PROBABLE carcinogen' in 2015, citing strong evidence of genotoxicity/oxidative stress. (B) The US EPA concluded glyphosate is 'NOT LIKELY to be carcinogenic to humans,' and the EU/EFSA re-approved it (2023). BUT on 17 Jun 2022 the 9th Circuit (NRDC v. EPA) VACATED the human-health portion of EPA's interim decision as inconsistent with the agency's own record and remanded; EPA later withdrew the interim decision. So the regulatory 'safe' finding is itself under court-ordered reconsideration. grade: the disagreement is real; both findings are fact; the carcinogenicity conclusion is NOT settled either way.
the_scotus_pivot: The 2026 turning point: in Monsanto v. Durnell (decided 25 Jun 2026, 7-2, Kavanaugh) the Supreme Court held the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA) EXPRESSLY PREEMPTS state-law failure-to-warn claims — reversing/remanding John Durnell's >$1M Missouri verdict (jury 2019, affirmed on appeal). Justice Jackson dissented (joined by Gorsuch). Bayer says the ruling should dismiss pending failure-to-warn claims, but it still plans to proceed with a proposed $7.25B class-action settlement (awaiting Missouri approval; jurisdictional dispute). grade: fact (the ruling, vote, dissent, and settlement posture).
the_state_shields: The legislative track running parallel to the courts: in 2025, North Dakota (HB 1318, the first, Apr 2025) and Georgia (SB 144) enacted laws making a FIFRA-registered, EPA-approved pesticide LABEL a COMPLETE DEFENSE to state failure-to-warn claims (with a carve-out if EPA finds the maker KNOWINGLY withheld/misrepresented safety info). Reporting (Investigate Midwest) documented a Bayer-backed multi-state push; a counter federal bill was proposed to establish a right to sue for pesticide injuries. grade: fact (the statutes + the documented campaign); 'shield vs accountability' is the value dispute.
the_liability_engineering_link: This is a THIRD distinct liability-containment mechanism for the corpus's Liability_Engineering thesis: not the bankruptcy two-step (J&J talc) nor non-consensual releases (Purdue/Sackler — which SCOTUS STRUCK in Harrington, 2024), but FEDERAL PREEMPTION (Durnell, 2026) + state label-defense STATUTES. Note the asymmetry, dated: the same Court that REFUSED to let Purdue use bankruptcy to extinguish victims' claims (2024) here EXPANDED a shield by reading FIFRA to bar state failure-to-warn (2026) — different doctrines, opposite directions. grade: structural comparison (overlay).
the_honest_reading: Roundup is a mass tort whose factual spine is solid (verdicts, ~177,000 claims, ~$11B+ paid) but whose central scientific question is genuinely UNSETTLED — IARC 'probable carcinogen' (2015) vs EPA 'not likely,' with the EPA finding itself vacated and remanded (2022). The 2026 SCOTUS Durnell ruling (FIFRA preemption, 7-2) is the decisive legal event: it removes the failure-to-warn theory that powered the tort, even as Bayer pursues a $7.25B settlement and states erect statutory label-defenses. For the Liability_Engineering thesis it is the preemption/statutory-shield variant — and a dated illustration that the same SCOTUS can both block (Purdue) and expand (Durnell) liability shields. Both sides retained with dates; overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: CNBC — Supreme Court limits Roundup cancer suits against Bayer's Monsanto (25 Jun 2026, 7-2)

Springer / Environmental Sciences Europe — how EPA and IARC reached opposite conclusions
North Dakota Monitor — ND pesticide-protection law (HB 1318), a US first (Apr 2025)
Investigate Midwest — inside the corporate push to limit pesticide liability (2025)

'not likely to be carcinogenic' — BUT 9th Cir (NRDC v EPA, 17 Jun 2022) VACATED the human-health finding as inconsistent + remanded; EPA withdrew the interim decision

Monsanto v. Durnell (7-2, 25 Jun 2026, Kavanaugh): FIFRA EXPRESSLY PREEMPTS state failure-to-warn; reversed Durnell's >$1M MO verdict; Jackson dissent (w/ Gorsuch) — should dismiss pending failure-to-warn claims
